content.full_text:
Re:
Fnrn Polrrrcer- PRACTrcps CoMMrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95811-2329
(916\ 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February 20,2009
Catherine L. DiCamillo
Office of the City Attomey
1901 Airporr Road, Sre. 300
South Lake Tahoe, California 96150-7051
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-006
Dear Ms. DiCamillo:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' Please note that our advice is based
solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if
any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interiit or
Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTTON
Is Councilmember Lovell prohibited by the Act from participating in a decision
on whether to appeal a ruling by a hearing board to reinstate a police officer who was
previously terminated by the City when her son is a police officer in the same police
department?
CONCLUSION
No. Councilmember Lovell is not prohibited by the Act from participating in a
decision on whether to appeal a ruling by a hearing board to reinstate a polici officer who
was previously terminated when her son is a police officer in the same police department.
' The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Governmenr Code Sections 81000 through 91014. AII
statutory references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 rhrough l8gg7 of Tirle 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory rel'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. 4-09-006
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are the City Attorney for the City of South Lake Tahoe and have requested
advice on behalf of Councilmember Kathay Lovell. tn Septemb er of 2007, ttre iity of
South Lake Tahoe dismissed a lO-year police officer. Councilmember Love11', 3g-year
old son Ryan was hired by the City of South Lake Tahoe as a police trainee on
January 23,2008. Upon his graduation from the Police Acaderny in June of 200g, Ryan
became a member of the Police officer's Association labor group.
The terminated police officer appealed the termination under the police Officers,
Association's Memorandum of Understanding with the City of South Lake Tahoe. A
three-member hearing board was appointed to hear the police officer's appeal of his
termination in a hearing held on August 20-2L,2008. The hearing board'iequested briefs
and took the matter under submission, issuing its Decision and Order on December I l,
2008. The hearing board granted in part and denied in part the officer's appeal, finding
that the appropriate level of discipline was a six week suspension without^pay, and
thereafter an eighteen month restriction on the officer's uUitity to volunteei fo. *y
position, duty, assignment, or activity that provides incentive pay, premium pay, or any
other pay above his salary base.
The city council is now considering whether to appeal the Board's Decision and
Order to the Superior Court. Councilmember Lovell wouid like to participate in funher
discussions regarding this matter provided doing so does not create a conflict of interest.
AI\ALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making
or in any way using or attempting to use his or her official posiiion to influen-ce a
governmental decision in which he or she knows or has reaion to know that he or she has
a disqualifying conflict of interest. Under Section 87103, a public official has a financial
interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the dicision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, or
on a member of his or her immediate family. To identify disqualifying conflicts of
interest, the Commis,sion has adopted a standard analysis, set tut in Regulation 1g700(b),
the pertinent steps of which we apply to your facts.
Step One: Is Councilmember Lovell a pubtic official?
The Act's conflicrof-interest provisions apply only to "public officials."
(Sections 87100 and 87103; Regulation 18700(bxf;.) "fubtic offi.ial', is defined as
"every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local govemment
agency . . .." (Section 82048.) As a member of the city council oithe City of South
Lake Tahoe, Councilmember Kathay Lovell is a public official, subject to ih. conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Act.


File No. A-09-006
Page No.3
Step Two: Is councilmember Lovell making, participating in making, or
influencing a governmental decision?
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only when a public official makes,
participates in making, or in any way attempts to use his or her offiiial position to
influence a govemmental decision in which the official knows - or has ieason to know -
that he or she has a financial interest. (Section 87100; Regulation 1g700(bx2).)
A public official "makes a governmental decision" when the official, acting
within the authority of his or her position, votes on a matter, obligates or commits his or
her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of
his or her agency. (Section 87100; Regulation 18702.1.) A public official "participates
in making a governmental decision" when, acting within the authority of his or hei
position and without significant substantive review, the official n"goiiute., advises or
makes recorlmendations to the decision maker regarding the governmental decision.
(Section 87100; Regulation 18702.2.) A public official is attJmpting to use his or her
official position to influence a decision before his or her own ug"ncy if, for the purpose
of influencing the decision, the official contacts or appears before any membe., offic".,
employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Section 87100; Regulation 1g702.3.)
councilmember Lovell's discussing and voting on a police department
employee's appeal is considered making and participating in making a govemmental
decision under the Act.
Step Three: What are the applicable economic interests?
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from certain enumerated economic interests. Theii economic interests are described in
Section 87103 and Regulations 18703-18703.5, inclusive:
o A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he
or she has a direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more. (Section gitOf (a);
Regulation 18703. l(a).)
o { public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or
she is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.
(Section 87103(d); Regulation 18703. l(b).)
o A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she
has a direct or indirecr interesr of $2,000 or more. (section sir0:16j; Regularion
18703.2.)
o An official has an economic interest in any source of income, including


File No. 4-09-006
Page No.4
promised income, totaling $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section
87103(c); Regulation I 8703.3.)
o A public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her
if the gifts total $420 or more within 12 months prior to thJ decision. (-section g7103(e);
Regulation 18703.4.)
o A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal expenses,
income, assets, or liabilities, as well as those of his or her immediaie family. tnir i,
commonly referred to as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87i03;
Regulation 18703.5.)
There is nothing in the facts you have provided that indicates Councilmember
Lovell has any of the above economic interests involved in the appeal of the Board's
Decision and Order to the Superior Court. Therefore, Councilmimber Lovell would not
be prohibited by the Act from participating in the govemmental decisions.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
SKBjgl
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
j'ldw R-b,,---
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, trgal Division

Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

CONCLUSION: No. Councilmember Lovell is not prohibited by the Act from participating in a
decision on whether to appeal a ruling by a hearing board to reinstate a polici officer who
